item.title: US stock futures slip as Middle East war de-escalation remains uncertain
item.description: U.S. stock index ​futures were down on Thursday, following gains in the previous session, as investors ‌remained cautious over developments in the Middle East while weighing the prospects of a de-escalation.

item.title: From beer to cosmetics, Asia feels full force of war-fuelled energy crisis
item.description: From beer and crisps to noodles, toys and cosmetics, companies - and consumers - across Asia are bracing for a crisis as the Iran war wreaks havoc on supply chains, plastics and oil supplies, upending everyday life and sending prices soaring.

item.title: Korean Air plans 103 Boeing plane purchases valued at $36.2 bln until 2039
item.description: South Korean ​flag carrier Korean ‌Air said on Thursday ​it plans ​to buy 103 ⁠Boeing planes ​between 2026 ​and 2039.

item.title: US lawmakers to introduce bill to ban government use of Chinese robots
item.description: Two U.S. senators on Thursday planned to introduce a bill that would ban the government from buying or operating humanoid robots made ​by Chinese firms.

item.title: Wall Street bonuses surge 9% to record $49.2 billion in 2025, NY comptroller says
item.description: Bonuses for Wall Street executives jumped 9% to a record $49.2 billion ​in 2025, according to an estimate from New ‌York State Comptroller Tom DiNapoli published on Thursday.

item.title: South Korea to invest $166 million in AI chip startup Rebellions
item.description: South Korea's industry ministry on Tuesday said the Financial Services Commission's advisory board ​approved a 250 billion won ($166 million) investment ‌in a local artificial intelligence chip startup called Rebellions, part of a government-backed push to nurture a homegrown advanced semiconductor ​firm.

item.title: Rohm, Toshiba, Mitsubishi Elec to begin power chip integration talks, Nikkei says
item.description: Japan's Rohm , Toshiba ​and Mitsubishi Electric ‌will start talks to integrate ​their power ​semiconductor businesses that can ⁠form the ​world's second-biggest power ​chip group after Infineon , the Nikkei ​newspaper said ​on Thursday.

item.title: Google top India counsel quits in latest departure amid regulatory hurdles, sources say
item.description: Google's top India counsel, Bijoya Roy, has resigned after 16 months ​in the role, two sources said, a ‌high-profile exit in a key market where the U.S. tech giant is facing regulatory hurdles and also lacks a ​government relations head.

item.title: Uber, Pony.ai and Verne team up to launch Europe's first robotaxi service in Croatia
item.description: Uber Technologies has partnered with Pony.ai and autonomous vehicle startup Verne to roll out the first commercial ​robotaxi service in Europe, with operations set to ‌start in the Croatian capital Zagreb.

item.title: Exclusive: China considers easing bank shareholding limits to boost capital
item.description: China is considering easing shareholding restrictions for some major investors, people with knowledge of the matter said, in a move aimed at broadening capital-raising options for commercial banks reeling ​from an economic slowdown.

item.title: Hair care brand Olaplex to be acquired by Germany's Henkel for $1.4 billion
item.description: Hair ​care brand ‌Olaplex Holdings ​on ​Thursday said ⁠it ​entered into ​a definitive agreement ​to ​be acquired ‌by ⁠Germany consumer goods ​maker ​Henkel ⁠for $1.4 billion.

item.title: Australia's Qantas boosts Europe flight as demand climbs due to Mideast war
item.description: Australia's flag carrier Qantas Airways QAN.AX said on Thursday it is adding flights to Rome and Paris to meet an ​upswing in demand for European routes, while global air ‌travel remains severely disrupted after the Middle East war forced the closure of major hubs, including Dubai and Doha.

item.title: Volkswagen owner Porsche SE reports blow to 2025 earnings
item.description: Porsche SE , which ​controls German ‌auto group Volkswagen , suffered a ​9% ​blow to its after-tax ⁠profit in ​2025, the ​company said on Wednesday.

item.title: H&M's Q1 profit grows more than expected, sees March sales up 1%
item.description: Swedish fashion retailer H&M reported ​on Thursday a slightly bigger rise than expected in ‌December-February operating profit, and predicted March sales would be up 1% in local currencies.

item.title: National Australia Bank to cut 170 jobs amid offshore expansion
item.description: Australia's Finance Sector Union said on Thursday that National Australia Bank is proposing cutting about 170 jobs across the country ​as part of a restructuring of its business division, which ‌will also create new roles offshore.

item.title: Morning Bid: Hope and Hormuz
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Ankur Banerjee

item.title: Japan shifts focus to oil in unorthodox scramble to talk up yen
item.description: Japan is weighing a controversial plan to arrest the yen's slide: stepping into oil futures markets, sources say, as long-standing policy tools lose traction ​against stubborn inflation pressures.

item.title: Revolut to base 40% of its global workforce in India by 2026
item.description: European fintech firm Revolut plans to have around 40% of its global workforce based in India by the end of 2026 as ​it expands its India global capability centre.

item.title: Trade ministers meet in Cameroon for critical WTO reform talks amid deep divisions
item.description: Trade ministers will meet in Cameroon on Thursday for crucial discussions on reforming the World Trade Organization, as some diplomats and trade officials warn that ​without an agreement, countries may set trade rules outside the organisation.

item.title: Nvidia-backed Reflection AI eyes $25 billion valuation, WSJ reports
item.description: Nvidia-backed startup Reflection AI is in talks to ​raise $2.5 billion at a ‌valuation of $25 billion, the Wall Street Journal reported on Wednesday, ​citing people familiar with ​the matter.

item.title: Stocks on edge as Middle East ceasefire talks take centre stage
item.description: Asian stocks struggled for direction while the dollar held firm on Thursday as investors treaded ​cautiously amid dizzying developments in the Middle East, where Iran said it would weigh a U.S. proposal to ‌end the Gulf conflict.

item.title: Germany's Henkel nears deal for hair care brand Olaplex, Bloomberg News reports (March 25)
item.description: German consumer goods ⁠maker Henkel is ​in advanced talks to ​buy Olaplex in a deal that would value the ​U.S. hair care ​products maker at about $2 per share, ‌Bloomberg ⁠News reported on Wednesday, citing sources.

item.title: Nanya Technology shares surge 10% after $2.5 billion private placement
item.description: Shares of ​memory chip ‌maker Nanya Technology opened ​on ​Thursday limit up 10% ⁠after ​it issued ​a $2.5 billion private placement backed ​by tech ​firms such as ‌Sandisk ⁠Technology.

item.title: Trump's tariffs had little impact on GDP in 2025, but raised revenue, academic paper finds
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump's barrage of tariffs last year had only a minimal impact on U.S. economic output but raised significant federal revenue and ​contributed to a further U.S.-China trade decoupling, a new Brookings Institution academic ‌paper showed on Wednesday.

item.title: Brookings Institution paper charts path toward smaller Fed balance sheet
item.description: If the Federal Reserve truly wants a smaller balance sheet, it can get there with regulatory changes, tweaks to the payment system and more frequent market interventions by the ​central bank, new research published by the Brookings Institution said on Wednesday.

item.title: Worker sues Valero over Texas refinery explosion
item.description: A man who said he was injured in an explosion at Valero Energy's Port Arthur, Texas refinery on Monday night ​filed a lawsuit on Wednesday in a state district court ‌alleging the company failed to properly maintain the refinery.

item.title: Hyundai Motor plans to launch 36 new models in North America by 2030
item.description: Hyundai Motor ​said on ‌Thursday it is targeting ​vehicle ​sales of 500,000 ⁠units in ​China this ​year, and plans to launch ​36 ​new models in the ‌North ⁠American market by 2030.

item.title: Japan's corporate service inflation perks up in February
item.description: A key gauge of Japan's service-sector inflation rose 2.7% in February from a ​year earlier, data showed on Thursday, reinforcing the ‌central bank's view that a tight labour market is pushing firms to pass rising costs on to consumers.

item.title: Brazil's Americanas files motion to exit bankruptcy protection proceedings
item.description: Brazilian retailer Americanas ​said on Wednesday it filed ‌a motion in a Rio de Janeiro court to exit bankruptcy protection proceedings, ​as the company claims it ​fulfilled all previously established obligations.

item.title: US oil prices rise as investors assess Middle East de-escalation
item.description: U.S. oil prices rose in early trade on Thursday, recovering some of the ​previous day's losses as investors assessed prospects ‌for de-escalation in the Middle East and Iran reviewed a U.S. proposal to end the war, which has ​disrupted energy flows from the Gulf.
